name: Grade 6 Language Arts — Semester 1
description: An 18-week Grade 6 pathway for claims, evidence, organization, research, citations, and academic discussion. Learning advances through inquiry, practice, authentic application, revision, and communication.
learningGoals: Develop grade 6 knowledge and transferable skills in claims, evidence, organization, research, citations, and academic discussion.
Use appropriate evidence, vocabulary, tools, and methods to explain reasoning.
Plan, revise, collaborate responsibly, and communicate a well-supported conclusion.

lessons.title: Launch inquiry: claims, evidence, organization, research, citations, and academic discussion
lessons.objective: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about claims, evidence, organization, research, citations, and academic discussion and explain my reasoning with appropriate evidence.

outcomes: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about claims, evidence, organization, research, citations, and academic discussion.

activities.title: Question and evidence
activities.learnerAction: The learner develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about claims, evidence, organization, research, citations, and academic discussion, documents evidence in an age-appropriate format, and follows applicable safety, ethics, and collaboration expectations.

successCriteria: The learner completes a grade 6 task connected to claims, evidence, organization, research, citations, and academic discussion.

The learner revises, seeks feedback, or identifies a specific next step.
